body.title: CLOSED: pe_chain confidence gate — fast-fail on hypothesis_error
body.result: Fast-fail confidence gate in pe_chain sprint flow: escalates immediately when hypothesis_error set after retries, before pe_test/pe_probe run
body.description:
pe_chain proceeds from HYPOTHESIZE to IMPLEMENT without verifying hypothesis validity. 'old_string not found in file' is the most common pe_chain failure mode and is entirely detectable before IMPLEMENT runs. A pre-IMPLEMENT confidence gate catches bad hypotheses before they cause damage.

After pe_hypothesize(), for each hypothesis check: (1) does old_string exist verbatim in the target file (grep -c)? (2) is the target file in the ticket's Affected files list? If either check fails, set basket['confidence_fail'] with reason and skip pe_implement() — escalate to CC inbox instead. This is mechanical, not LLM-based.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (MEDIUM inertia)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/coding (MEDIUM inertia; pe_chain is core execution pipeline — gate shape discussed here); no sqlite
**Scope boundary:** In scope: post-HYPOTHESIZE mechanical check: old_string grep + affected-files membership. Out of scope: LLM quality scoring of hypothesis coherence (future ticket); changes to HYPOTHESIZE prompt.
**Test plan:** When pe_hypothesize() produces old_string='nonexistent text', confidence gate fires and pe_implement() is not called. CC inbox receives escalation with reason 'old_string not found in file'. Existing passing tests unchanged. Ticket T-igor-pec-confidence-gate closes the most common pe_chain failure mode.
